facts: The muscular system and skeletal system work together to move the body because muscles pull on bones, not because they push.
The eye is a complex sensory organ that detects light, not sound, and sends signals to the nervous system for vision.
The ear is a complex sensory organ that detects vibrations in the air, not light, and sends signals to the nervous system for hearing.
The nervous system coordinates muscles and senses by sending signals from the brain to the body, not by muscles acting on their own.
Glasses correct eyesight by bending light before it enters the eye, not by changing the shape of the eye itself.
misconceptions: Students think muscles can push bones to move them, but muscles only pull on bones to create movement.
Students believe the ear hears by detecting light or that the eye sees by detecting sound, when each organ is specialized for a different type of signal.
